value_picks.name: WesBanco, Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Regional bank trading at a large discount to fair value in April 2026, with investor caution around credit quality and rate-cycle uncertainty despite a stable core deposit franchise.
value_picks.bull_case: As rate volatility normalizes and credit remains contained, earnings power and dividend support a re-rating toward peer multiples; any M&A optionality could further unlock value.

value_picks.name: DNOW Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Energy/industrial distribution name highlighted as materially undervalued versus cash-flow-based fair value in mid-April 2026, likely reflecting cyclical end-market concerns.
value_picks.bull_case: If energy capex remains resilient and margin initiatives stick, DNOW can compound free cash flow and return capital, driving multiple expansion.

value_picks.name: FB Financial Corporation
value_picks.why_undervalued: Bank shares screened as deeply discounted to cash-flow-based fair value in April 2026 while the company reported strong Q1 2026 profitability, suggesting the market was still pricing in an overly bearish credit scenario.
value_picks.bull_case: Sustained NIM stability and disciplined expense control could keep ROE elevated and support a re-rating as recession fears fade.

value_picks.name: Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation
value_picks.why_undervalued: The stock was hitting 52-week lows on April 24, 2026 amid ongoing concerns about slower IT services spending and pricing pressure.
value_picks.bull_case: If enterprise spend rebounds and management delivers on efficiency/AI-led services mix, Cognizant’s high cash generation could support buybacks and a valuation reset.

value_picks.name: Charter Communications, Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Charter appeared on 52-week-lows lists around April 24, 2026 as broadband subscriber pressures and competitive fiber buildouts weighed on sentiment.
value_picks.bull_case: Stabilizing net adds plus ongoing share repurchases and improving mobile economics could drive a multi-year FCF inflection and rerating.

momentum_picks.name: MaxLinear, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Shares surged after Q1 2026 results and a wave of analyst target hikes/upgrades in late April 2026, pushing the stock toward new 52-week highs.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: High upside if earnings momentum continues, but elevated drawdown risk given extreme short-term run-up and historical volatility.

momentum_picks.name: Kaiser Aluminum Corporation
momentum_picks.catalyst: Strong price momentum into late April 2026, with Zacks highlighting outsized 1-week/1-month gains versus industry amid improving estimates and cyclical optimism.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Moderate upside with cyclical leverage, but exposed to aluminum demand swings and margin compression if end-markets soften.

momentum_picks.name: Astera Labs, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: AI infrastructure connectivity leader continued a sharp rally into late April 2026 as hyperscaler/datacenter enthusiasm stayed strong and shares traded near $200+ levels.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Strong secular tailwinds, but valuation risk is high and any AI capex pause could compress multiples quickly.

momentum_picks.name: Credo Technology Group Holding Ltd
momentum_picks.catalyst: High-speed connectivity/optical interconnect narrative drove a sustained uptrend through April 2026, supported by positive news flow and rising institutional interest.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Attractive trend strength, but momentum-driven positioning makes the stock vulnerable to sharp reversals on any negative guidance.

momentum_picks.name: Enovix Corporation
momentum_picks.catalyst: Battery-tech name stayed active into late April 2026 ahead of upcoming Q1 2026 earnings timing, with market attention on commercialization progress.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Potentially asymmetric if execution improves, but product/scale risk remains high and losses can weigh on sentiment.

watchlist_updates.detail: Intel shares soared 23.6% on Friday after reporting stronger-than-expected Q1 results.
United Rentals up 23% on blowout earnings and raised FY guidance.
Texas Instruments gained 19% on upbeat revenue outlook.
West Pharmaceutical up 13% on strong demand outlook.
Molina Healthcare rose 6% on stable insurance margins.
CSX up 5.5% on freight demand strength.
